docs.title: Home
docs.text:
# portapkg **Portable, offline-friendly Python package bundler and installer.** Bundle a package and all its dependencies on an online machine, then install on an air-gapped machine with nothing but Python and pip. 🗜️ No server needed

Bundles are plain folders — copy them on a USB drive and you're done.

🌐 Multi-platform

One bundle works across Windows, Linux, macOS, x86, and ARM.

🧩 Self-contained

The standalone portapkg.py is stdlib only — zero dependencies.

📦 Export & ship

One command creates a portable folder with everything you need.

docs.title: 🔵 On an online machine
docs.text:
pip install portapkg
portapkg bundle instrumation

This creates a bundles/ folder with wheels for all 6 platforms × 5 Python versions.

docs.title: 🟢 On an offline machine
docs.text:
Copy bundles/ and portapkg.py to a USB drive, then run:

python portapkg.py list
python portapkg.py install instrumation

docs.title: 🟠 Export as a portable package
docs.text:
portapkg export instrumation

Creates instrumation_a3f2e6_2026-05-26/ with portapkg.py + bundles/ inside — ready to ship.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
1. Dependency resolution — pip download <package> resolves the full transitive dependency tree using pip's own resolver.
  2. Multi-platform fetch — For each dependency, wheels are downloaded across 6 platforms × 5 Python versions.
  3. Fallback — If no binary wheel exists for a platform, it falls back to a source distribution with a warning.
  4. Offline install — pip install --no-index --find-links <wheels_dir> installs from local files only.

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
• Usage guide — detailed CLI and standalone instructions
  • CLI reference — full command reference
  • Export bundles — package everything into one portable folder
  • Configuration — environment variables and options
  • Development — contributing, testing, project structure

docs.title: portapkg
docs.text: The main CLI for bundling packages on an online machine.

docs.title: portapkg bundle <package>
docs.text:
Bundle a package for offline install.

portapkg bundle instrumation
portapkg bundle instrumation --platforms win_amd64,macosx_13_0_arm64
portapkg bundle instrumation --snapshot

Options:

Flag Description package Package name (positional, required) --platforms Comma-separated platform tags (default: all 6) --snapshot Snapshot current environment (single-platform)

docs.title: portapkg list
docs.text:
List all bundles in the bundles directory.

portapkg list

docs.title: portapkg info <package>
docs.text:
Show details about a specific bundle.

portapkg info instrumation

docs.title: portapkg export <package>
docs.text:
Bundle + standalone installer into a portable folder.

portapkg export instrumation
portapkg export instrumation --output ./dist

Creates {package}_{random_id}_{date}/ with portapkg.py + bundles/{package}/ inside — ready to ship.

Options:

Flag Description package Package name (positional, required) --output, -o Output directory (default: current directory)

docs.title: portapkg update <package>
docs.text:
Re-fetch a bundle (same options as bundle).

portapkg update instrumation
portapkg update instrumation --platforms win_amd64
portapkg update instrumation --snapshot

Options:

Flag Description package Package name (positional, required) --platforms Comma-separated platform tags --snapshot Snapshot mode

docs.title: Global options
docs.text: • PORTAPKG_BUNDLES_DIR environment variable overrides the bundle directory

docs.title: portapkg.py
docs.text: The standalone script — works for both bundling and installing on any machine with Python and pip. No pip install portapkg needed.

docs.title: python portapkg.py bundle <package>
docs.text:
Bundle a package (same options as the CLI).

python portapkg.py bundle instrumation
python portapkg.py bundle instrumation --platforms win_amd64,macosx_13_0_arm64
python portapkg.py bundle instrumation --snapshot

docs.title: python portapkg.py export <package>
docs.text:
Export a bundle into a portable folder.

python portapkg.py export instrumation
python portapkg.py export instrumation --output ./dist

docs.title: python portapkg.py install <package>
docs.text:
Install a bundled package.

python portapkg.py install instrumation
python portapkg.py install instrumation --target ./venv/Lib/site-packages

Options:

Flag Description package Package name (positional, required) --target Custom install path

docs.title: python portapkg.py list
docs.text:
List available bundles.

python portapkg.py list

docs.title: Global options
docs.text: • PORTAPKG_BUNDLES_DIR environment variable overrides the bundle directory

docs.title: PORTAPKG_BUNDLES_DIR
docs.text:
Overrides the default bundle storage directory.

Default: ./bundles/ (relative to the current working directory)

Example:

export PORTAPKG_BUNDLES_DIR=/data/portapkg/bundles
portapkg bundle instrumation
# stores in /data/portapkg/bundles/instrumation/

Both the CLI (portapkg) and the standalone script (portapkg.py) respect this environment variable.

docs.title: Default platforms and Python versions
docs.text:
When no --platforms flag is given, the bundler uses all 6 platforms:

Platform Tag Windows x86_64 win_amd64 Windows x86 win32 Linux x86_64 manylinux2014_x86_64 Linux ARM64 manylinux2014_aarch64 macOS Apple Silicon macosx_13_0_arm64 macOS Intel macosx_13_0_x86_64

And all 5 Python versions: 3.9, 3.10, 3.11, 3.12, 3.13

docs.title: Bundle structure
docs.text:
A typical bundle looks like this:

bundles/
└── instrumation/
    ├── manifest.json
    └── wheels/
        ├── instrumation-0.3.0-py3-none-any.whl
        ├── pyserial-3.5-py2.py3-none-any.whl
        └── ...

docs.title: manifest.json
docs.text:
Contains metadata about the bundle:

{
  "name": "instrumation",
  "version": "0.3.0",
  "date_bundled": "2026-05-26T12:00:00",
  "source_platform": "macosx_13_0_arm64",
  "source_python": "314",
  "dependencies": [
    {"name": "instrumation", "version": "0.3.0"},
    {"name": "pyserial", "version": "3.5"}
  ]
}

docs.title: Project structure
docs.text:
portapkg/
├── portapkg.py                ← standalone offline installer (stdlib only)
├── portapkg/
│   ├── __init__.py            ← version
│   ├── cli.py                 ← entrypoint: bundle/list/info/update
│   ├── bundler/
│   │   ├── fetch.py           ← multi-platform wheel download
│   │   ├── manifest.py        ← manifest.json read/write
│   │   └── resolver.py        ← dependency resolution
│   └── installer/
│       ├── install.py         ← offline install logic
│       └── platform.py        ← OS/arch/python detection
├── tests/                     ← pytest test suite
├── docs/                      ← documentation site (MkDocs)
├── pyproject.toml             ← packaging & tool config
└── .github/workflows/         ← CI / release / docs workflows

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/abduznik/portapkg-py
cd portapkg-py

# Install in editable mode with dev dependencies
pip install -e ".[dev]"

# Install docs dependencies (optional)
pip install mkdocs mkdocs-material

docs.title: Running tests
docs.text:
# Run all tests
pytest

# With coverage
pytest --cov=portapkg --cov-report=term-missing

# Run a specific test file
pytest tests/test_platform.py

# Run a specific test
pytest tests/test_platform.py::TestParseWheelFilename

docs.title: Code style
docs.text:
This project uses Ruff for linting.

pip install ruff
ruff check portapkg/ tests/ portapkg.py

docs.title: Building documentation
docs.text:
# Preview locally
mkdocs serve

# Build static site
mkdocs build

The documentation is automatically deployed to GitHub Pages on push to main via .github/workflows/docs.yml.

docs.title: Making a release
docs.text:
1. Bump the version in portapkg/__init__.py and pyproject.toml
  2. Commit and tag: git tag v0.x.x && git push --tags
  3. Trigger the Publish workflow on GitHub, specifying the version tag

The workflow builds wheels + sdist, publishes to PyPI, and creates a GitHub Release with the artifacts attached.

docs.title: Design principles
docs.text:
• Stdlib only — no third-party imports anywhere in the runtime code
  • Single-file fallback — portapkg.py must work with nothing but Python and pip
  • Pip as engine — all heavy lifting (download, resolve, install) delegates to pip; portapkg just orchestrates
  • Clear errors — every failure mode has a human-readable message with a suggested fix

docs.title: Exporting bundles
docs.text: The export command packages a bundle and the standalone installer into a single portable folder — ready to copy to a USB drive and ship.

docs.title: Why export?
docs.text:
Instead of manually copying portapkg.py and the bundles/ directory, export creates a tidy, timestamped folder that includes everything the offline machine needs:

instrumation_a3f2e6_2026-05-26/
├── portapkg.py          ← standalone installer (stdlib only)
└── bundles/
    └── instrumation/
        ├── manifest.json
        └── wheels/
            └── ...

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
You must have already bundled the package before exporting it.

# Export a bundle for distribution
portapkg export instrumation

# Export to a specific directory
portapkg export instrumation --output ./dist

docs.title: How the folder is named
docs.text:
The export folder is named automatically as:

{package}_{short_id}_{today_date}
Part Description Example package The package name instrumation short_id A random 6-character hex string a3f2e6 today_date Today's date in ISO format 2026-05-26

docs.title: What's inside
docs.text: File / folder Purpose portapkg.py The standalone offline installer — no dependencies, stdlib only bundles/{package}/ The bundle manifest and wheels bundles/{package}/manifest.json Metadata (version, deps, platform info) bundles/{package}/wheels/ All downloaded wheel files

docs.title: Using the exported folder on an offline machine
docs.text:
# Copy the entire folder to the offline machine, then:

cd instrumation_a3f2e6_2026-05-26/

# List available bundles
python portapkg.py list

# Install the package (no internet needed)
python portapkg.py install instrumation

docs.title: Using portapkg.py from source as an alternative
docs.text:
If you don't want to pip install portapkg on the online machine, you can use the portapkg.py file directly from the source repository:

# Clone the repo
git clone https://github.com/abduznik/portapkg-py
cd portapkg-py

# Use the module directly (no pip install needed!)
python3 -m portapkg.cli bundle instrumation
python3 -m portapkg.cli list
python3 -m portapkg.cli info instrumation

# Export using the standalone file
PORTAPKG_BUNDLES_DIR=./bundles python3 portapkg.py export instrumation

No install required

The portapkg.py script is fully self-contained. You can use it both for bundling (on an online machine with pip) and installing (on an offline machine with pip). Just make sure pip is available on both ends.

docs.title: Environment variables
docs.text: • PORTAPKG_BUNDLES_DIR — override the bundle directory (default: ./bundles/)

docs.title: Standalone script (portapkg.py)
docs.text: The portapkg.py file is a fully self-contained offline installer that uses only the Python standard library (plus pip via subprocess). It has zero third-party dependencies.

docs.title: Why a standalone script?
docs.text:
• No pip install needed — just copy the file and run it
  • Works everywhere — any machine with Python 3.9+ and pip
  • Single file — easy to distribute, audit, or modify
  • Same bundle format — compatible with bundles created by the full CLI

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
# Copy portapkg.py alongside your bundles/ folder
# Then on the offline machine:

# List available bundles
python portapkg.py list

# Install a bundled package
python portapkg.py install instrumation

# Install to a custom path
python portapkg.py install instrumation --target ./venv/Lib/site-packages

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
1. Reads the bundle's manifest.json to understand the package structure
  2. Scans the wheels/ directory for wheels compatible with the current platform and Python version
  3. Runs pip install --no-index --find-links <wheels_dir> <package> to install from local files only

docs.title: Platform detection
docs.text:
The script detects the current OS and CPU architecture to select compatible wheels:

Machine Platform reported Windows x86_64 win_amd64 Windows x86 win32 Linux x86_64 linux_x86_64 Linux ARM64 linux_aarch64 macOS Apple Silicon macosx_13_0_arm64 macOS Intel macosx_13_0_x86_64

docs.title: Environment variables
docs.text: • PORTAPKG_BUNDLES_DIR — override the bundle directory (default: ./bundles/)

docs.title: From PyPI (recommended)
docs.text: pip install portapkg

docs.title: From source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/abduznik/portapkg-py
cd portapkg-py
pip install -e ".[dev]"

docs.title: No install needed — use portapkg.py directly
docs.text:
The portapkg.py file is fully self-contained — stdlib only, zero dependencies. You can use it for both bundling and installing without running pip install:

# Bundle a package (works if pip is available)
python3 portapkg.py bundle instrumation

# List bundles
python3 portapkg.py list

# Show bundle details  
python3 portapkg.py info instrumation

# Export for distribution
python3 portapkg.py export instrumation

One file to rule them all

portapkg.py works on both the online machine (bundling) and the offline machine (installing). Just copy it alongside your bundles/ folder and you're ready.

docs.title: Basic bundling
docs.text:
portapkg bundle instrumation

This downloads wheels for 6 platforms × 5 Python versions (up to 30 downloads per dependency). The output goes to ./bundles/instrumation/.

docs.title: Specific platforms
docs.text:
portapkg bundle pyserial --platforms win_amd64,macosx_13_0_arm64

Valid platform tags:

Platform Tag Windows x86_64 win_amd64 Windows x86 win32 Linux x86_64 manylinux2014_x86_64 Linux ARM64 manylinux2014_aarch64 macOS Apple Silicon macosx_13_0_arm64 macOS Intel macosx_13_0_x86_64

docs.title: Snapshot mode (single platform)
docs.text:
portapkg bundle instrumation --snapshot

Downloads wheels for your current machine only. Faster and more reliable, but the bundle only works on machines with the same OS and architecture.

docs.title: Custom bundle directory
docs.text: PORTAPKG_BUNDLES_DIR=/path/to/bundles portapkg bundle instrumation

docs.title: List and inspect bundles
docs.text:
# List all bundles
portapkg list

# Show details of a specific bundle
portapkg info instrumation

docs.title: Update a bundle
docs.text:
# Re-fetch a bundle with all platforms
portapkg update instrumation

# Re-fetch with specific platforms
portapkg update instrumation --platforms win_amd64,linux_x86_64

# Re-fetch as snapshot
portapkg update instrumation --snapshot

docs.title: Export a bundle for distribution
docs.text:
# Export the bundle + portapkg.py into a portable folder
portapkg export instrumation

# Export to a specific directory
portapkg export instrumation --output ./dist

This creates a timestamped folder like instrumation_a3f2e6_2026-05-26/ containing portapkg.py and the bundles/ folder — ready to zip up or copy to a USB drive.

docs.title: Installing offline
docs.text: Copy the bundles/ folder and portapkg.py to the offline machine.

docs.title: List available bundles
docs.text: python portapkg.py list

docs.title: Install a bundled package
docs.text: python portapkg.py install instrumation

docs.title: Install to a custom path
docs.text: python portapkg.py install instrumation --target ./venv/Lib/site-packages

docs.title: Custom bundle directory
docs.text:
PORTAPKG_BUNDLES_DIR=/path/to/bundles python portapkg.py list
PORTAPKG_BUNDLES_DIR=/path/to/bundles python portapkg.py install instrumation

docs.title: Typical workflow
docs.text: Online machine USB stick Offline machine portapkg bundle pyserial bundles/ folder python portapkg.py list portapkg bundle pyvisa portapkg.py file python portapkg.py install pyserial portapkg export pyserial pkg_abc123_2026-05-26/ folder unzip → python portapkg.py install pyserial → bundles ready in ./bundles/ Copy both → installs from local wheels

docs.title: Using portapkg.py without pip install
docs.text:
You can use the standalone portapkg.py from the source repository directly for both bundling and installing — no pip install required:

git clone https://github.com/abduznik/portapkg-py
cd portapkg-py

# Bundle a package (needs pip available)
python3 portapkg.py bundle instrumation

# Export for distribution
python3 portapkg.py export instrumation

# On the offline machine:
python3 portapkg.py install instrumation

portapkg.py is fully self-contained (stdlib only). The bundle and export commands delegate to pip under the hood, so they need pip available on the machine — but no portapkg-specific dependencies are needed.
